title: Persona Voice Card v1.0
description: A closed-vocabulary voice record substituted into a code-owned template. Every field is an enum or a length/character-bounded string — there is no freeform field a persona author can use to encode a decision policy (authorization tiers, verification-skip instructions, priority orderings). additionalProperties:false everywhere: adding a new field to this schema is a re-opened content channel and should be reviewed as a security change, not a routine schema tweak. This is the published mirror of the enforcement code in bin/lib/persona-voice-card.js — that file is authoritative for code-abyss's own runtime; this schema is for external tooling/community submissions to validate against.

label.description: UI/picker display only — never dereferenced by the render path.

description.description: UI/picker display only — never dereferenced by the render path.

self.description: Rendered into the prompt via a fixed template — self-address term.

user.description: Rendered into the prompt via a fixed template — user-address term.

language.description: Rendered into the prompt via a fixed template — short free-text phrasing/mixing style descriptor. Bounded and banned-char-filtered like self/user rather than a rigid enum, since real personas need more nuance than a 5-value category (e.g. 'literary skeleton, vernacular flesh, keep technical terms in English') and none of that nuance is judgment-shaped.

register.description: Selects one of five code-owned template sentences (bin/lib/persona-voice-card.js REGISTER_SENTENCES) — the persona picks, never writes, the sentence.

emoji_policy.description: Selects one of four code-owned template sentences (EMOJI_SENTENCES).

flourish.description: Short decorative phrases (catchphrases), rendered as blockquotes after the voice line. self.length + user.length + sum(flourish item lengths) must not exceed 60 total (enforced in code, not expressible in this schema alone) — this closes the 'split a table across individually-compliant array items' loophole. The budget sits below the per-field caps' own ceiling (16+16+2x32=96) so it is a real, binding constraint rather than a decorative one.

creator.description: UI-only metadata — never dereferenced by the render path.

license.description: UI-only metadata — never dereferenced by the render path.

tags.description: UI-only metadata — never dereferenced by the render path.

sample_prompts.description: UI-only metadata (picker preview copy, renamed from the old spec's conversation_starters) — never dereferenced by the render path.

definitions._permanently_absent_note: identity/behavior/style file pointers, capabilities (domains/expertise_level/authorization tiers), scenarios (name/triggers/chain/priority), and extensions existed in tech-persona-card v1.0 and are permanently removed here, not merely deprecated — additionalProperties:false rejects them outright. No field in this schema accepts a newline, a multi-clause sentence, or an ordering; there is no slot shaped like a decision table anywhere in the type.
